perturbation.original_text: FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its sole discretion, shall choose. FIIOC may change the format and/or content of such informational reports, and the frequency with which such information is provided.
perturbation.changed_text: FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its sole discretion, shall choose. FIIOC is required to provide such information at least quarterly and cannot change the format and/or content of such informational reports without prior written consent from the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding FIIOC's ability to modify the information provided. The original text grants FIIOC sole discretion to change the format and content. The modified text then imposes a restriction, requiring prior written consent from the Company before any changes can be made. This creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear whether FIIOC has unilateral control over the information's content and format.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information to be Provided to Company. FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its sole discretion, shall choose. FIIOC is required to provide such information at least quarterly and cannot change the format and/or content of such informational reports without prior written consent from the Company. FIIOC may change the format and/or content of such informational reports, and the frequency with which such information is provided.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants FIIOC sole discretion to change the format and content of information. The modified text contradicts this by requiring prior written consent from the Company before changes can be made. The Contradicted text contains both statements, resulting in a clear in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: FIIOC may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to Company (1) if required by any applicable law or regulation, (2) if so required by action of the Fund(s) Board of Trustees, or (3) if Company engages in any material breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: FIIOC may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to Company (1) if required by any applicable law or regulation, (2) if so required by action of the Fund(s) Board of Trustees, (3) if Company engages in any breach of this Agreement, or (4) at any time, for any reason, without notice or cause.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a new condition under which FIIOC can terminate the agreement: "at any time, for any reason, without notice or cause." This directly contradicts the prior clauses that specify limited conditions for immediate termination. The original clauses imply that termination requires a specific reason (legal requirement, Board action, or material breach). The added clause provides an unconditional right to terminate, making the specific conditions redundant and contradictory. This creates significant uncertainty about termination rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. This Agreement may be terminated by Company at any time upon written notice to FIIOC. FIIOC may terminate this Agreement at any time upon ninety (90) days' written notice to Company. FIIOC may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to Company (1) if required by any applicable law or regulation, (2) if so required by action of the Fund(s) Board of Trustees, (3) if Company engages in any breach of this Agreement, or (4) at any time, for any reason, without notice or cause. This Agreement shall terminate immediately and automatically upon the termination of Company's Participation Agreement(s) with the Funds, and in such event no notice need be given hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the clause "at any time, for any reason, without notice or cause" as a condition for FIIOC to terminate the agreement. This directly contradicts the original clause requiring 90 days' written notice for termination by FIIOC found in the Contradicted Text: "FIIOC may terminate this Agreement at any time upon ninety (90) days' written notice to Company." This creates an in-text contradiction, as FIIOC now has the option to terminate with or without notice, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the termination clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned without the written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, except that it shall be assigned automatically to any successor to FIIOC as the Funds' transfer agent, and any such successor shall be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be assigned without the written consent of the other party, which consent can be withheld for any reason, except that it shall be assigned automatically to any successor to FIIOC as the Funds' transfer agent, and any such successor shall not be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that consent to assignment cannot be 'unreasonably withheld.' The changed clause contradicts this by stating consent 'can be withheld for any reason.' Additionally, the original text bound FIIOC's successors to the agreement terms, while the change states they 'shall not be bound.' This creates internal contradictions within the assignment clause. It makes it unclear whether there are any limitations on withholding consent and what obligations FIIOC's successors have under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned without the written consent of the other party, which consent can be withheld for any reason, except that it shall be assigned automatically to any successor to FIIOC as the Funds' transfer agent, and any such successor shall not be bound by the terms of this Agreement. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have set their hands as of the date first written above. FIDELITY INVESTMENTS INSTITUTIONAL OPERATIONS COMPANY, INC. By: NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE AND ANNUITY CORPORATION By: Name: Title:
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text stated that consent to assignment could not be unreasonably withheld, and the successor shall be bound by the terms of this agreement. The change states consent can be withheld for any reason, and the successor shall not be bound by the terms of this agreement, which creates a clear contradiction within the same clause concerning assignment. This creates an in-text contradiction, making the obligations unclear. The contradicted text is in the same place as the Changed Text, but the changes introduce conflicting implications.
